Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Continued To Apr-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Loren Lopin, via ZOOM, for Nancy Lewellen, Trustee; Sheila Robello, via ZOOM, for Barbara Simon, Successor Trustee; Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, for Matthew Nestel, Beneficiary; Michael Freedman, via ZOOM, for William Nestel, Beneficiary.

The court advises that it is inclined to continue this matter to allow for the filing of an amended accounting petition. Mr. Lopin reports that the assets are not trust assets, therefore, are not subject to accounting and points to probate code 16069 (b) in support of his position. Mr. Lopin offers additional advocacy on behalf of his position. Mr. Freedman agrees with the court's inclination to continue today's hearing and summarizes what he and his client hoping to be resolved. Petition Seeking Court Order Determining The Validity Of Modification/Amendments To Trust Provision
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Continued To Jan-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Theodore E. Bacon for Harryette B. Nestel. Trisha Friedeberg for and with Matthew Nestel (Objector). Deborah Fox for Neil Fisher. June Levy and Azmi Baharudin (Friends of the Harryette Nestel) were also present.

The Court stated that before it today is a petition filed by the Trustor which includes the issue of capacity, that objections were filed by Mr. Fisher and Mr. Nestel, and that it understands that the parties have reached an agreement regarding the ongoing funding issues. Mr. Bacon stated that there is an initial proposal, that the issue of competence should be determined, that they believe that Mrs. Nestel has capacity, that there are objections, that they have retained a bookkeeper, that they have come to an agreement to pay the bills, and that they can set up a nurse practioner for visits for Ms. Nestel. Mr. Bacon also stated that he'll bring in the doctor, that seven declarations were filed last night, that they will have ten to fifteen witnesses, that some discovery will need to completed, and that he would go to mediation but not on the competency issue. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the petition should be denied on its face, that they are filing for conservatorship, that everyone is extremely concerned, that Mrs. Nestel is being taken advantage of by others, and that the conservatorship has to happen soon. The Court stated that the matter is not ready to be resolved today and that it would continue the petition before it today to allow time for the other procedural matters to play out. Mr. Bacon stated that the stipulation has been signed. The Court stated that no petition to approve any stipulation was before it today. Mr. Bacon stated that it was approved at an ex parte hearing. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the stipulation re: funding was approved at an ex parte hearing last Tuesday. Mr. Bacon stated that the stipulation was filed, that the order was submitted for signature, that there is an urgency as Mrs. Nestel is completely out of money, and that there will need to be a full evidentiary hearing in 90-120 days. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the accounts at Wells Fargo have been frozen and that for Mrs. Nestel's well-being that they will be filing for temporary conservatorship. The Court continued the Petition Seeking Court Order Determining The Validity of Modification/Amendments to Trust and Confirming Capacity to January 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see where things stand, to see how it all works out under the stipulation, and noted that at that time it will know how things are proceeding. Ms. Fox stated that her client was accused of not performing and that they presented their accounting. Brief discussions are held. Ms. Fox stated that if a conservator is appointed that the conservator can approve the accounting of a prior trustee. Mr. Bacon stated that could not be done. The Court stated that is not a debate to get into today and that the Court will not take action on an accounting that shows up as exhibits to an objection. Ms. Friedeberg noted that Mrs. Nestel's long time friends of over fifty years were present in court today, that when Mrs. Nestel's friends have attempted to visit her that the door is not answered, and that none of their phone calls to Mrs. Nestel are answered. Mr. Bacon waived notice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: M. Nuque. Matter not reported.

Ms. Navia stated that $40,000.00 is excessive. Ms. Fox inquired if the funds would be released to Ms. Nestel or a fiduciary, and stated this matter involved adult protective services. The Court sets a hearing date for an ex parte application to be heard on Tuesday, October 15, 2019 at 10:30 a.m. in Dept 202. Mr. Bacon to file his moving papers as soon as possible but no later than this Friday. Notice is waived.
